memberdef.name: ESP_INTR_FLAG_LEVEL1
memberdef.initializer: (1<<1) /* Accept a Level 1 int vector, lowest priority */

memberdef.name: ESP_INTR_FLAG_NMI
memberdef.initializer: (1<<7) /* Accept a Level 7 int vector, highest priority */

memberdef.name: ESP_INTR_FLAG_SHARED
memberdef.initializer: (1<<8) /* Interrupt can be shared between ISRs */

memberdef.name: ESP_INTR_FLAG_IRAM
memberdef.initializer: (1<<10) /* ISR can be called if cache is disabled */

briefdescription.para: Mark an interrupt as a shared interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: This will mark a certain interrupt on the specified CPU as an interrupt that can be used to hook shared interrupt handlers to.

parameterdescription.para: The number of the interrupt (0-31)
CPU on which the interrupt should be marked as shared (0 or 1)
Shared interrupt is for handlers that reside in IRAM and the int can be left enabled while the flash cache is disabled.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if cpu or intno is invalid 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Reserve an interrupt to be used outside of this framework.

detaileddescription.para: This will mark a certain interrupt on the specified CPU as reserved, not to be allocated for any reason.

parameterdescription.para: The number of the interrupt (0-31)
CPU on which the interrupt should be marked as shared (0 or 1)

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if cpu or intno is invalid 0 otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (int source, int flags, intr_handler_t handler, void *arg, intr_handle_t *ret_handle)
memberdef.name: esp_intr_alloc

briefdescription.para: Allocate an interrupt with the given parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This finds an interrupt that matches the restrictions as given in the flags parameter, maps the given interrupt source to it and hooks up the given interrupt handler (with optional argument) as well. If needed, it can return a handle for the interrupt as well.
The interrupt will always be allocated on the core that runs this function.
If ESP_INTR_FLAG_IRAM flag is used, and handler address is not in IRAM or RTC_FAST_MEM, then ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG is returned.

parameterdescription.para: The interrupt source. One of the *_INTR_SOURCE interrupt mux sources, as defined in esp-xtensa-intmux.h, or one of the internal ETS_INTERNAL_*_INTR_SOURCE sources as defined in this header.

An ORred mask of the ESP_INTR_FLAG_* defines. These restrict the choice of interrupts that this routine can choose from. If this value is 0, it will default to allocating a non-shared interrupt of level 1, 2 or 3. If this is ESP_INTR_FLAG_SHARED, it will allocate a shared interrupt of level 1. Setting ESP_INTR_FLAG_INTRDISABLED will return from this function with the interrupt disabled.
The interrupt handler. Must be NULL when an interrupt of level >3 is requested, because these types of interrupts aren't C-callable.
Optional argument for passed to the interrupt handler
Pointer to an intr_handle_t pointer to store a handle that can later be used to request details or free the interrupt. Can be NULL if no handle is required.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if the combination of arguments is invalid. -ENODEV No free interrupt found with the specified flags 0 otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (int source, int flags, uint32_t intrstatusreg, uint32_t intrstatusmask, intr_handler_t handler, void *arg, intr_handle_t *ret_handle)
memberdef.name: esp_intr_alloc_intrstatus

briefdescription.para: Allocate an interrupt with the given parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This essentially does the same as esp_intr_alloc, but allows specifying a register and mask combo. For shared interrupts, the handler is only called if a read from the specified register, ANDed with the mask, returns non-zero. By passing an interrupt status register address and a fitting mask, this can be used to accelerate interrupt handling in the case a shared interrupt is triggered; by checking the interrupt statuses first, the code can decide which ISRs can be skipped

parameterdescription.para: The interrupt source. One of the *_INTR_SOURCE interrupt mux sources, as defined in esp-xtensa-intmux.h, or one of the internal ETS_INTERNAL_*_INTR_SOURCE sources as defined in this header.

An ORred mask of the ESP_INTR_FLAG_* defines. These restrict the choice of interrupts that this routine can choose from. If this value is 0, it will default to allocating a non-shared interrupt of level 1, 2 or 3. If this is ESP_INTR_FLAG_SHARED, it will allocate a shared interrupt of level 1. Setting ESP_INTR_FLAG_INTRDISABLED will return from this function with the interrupt disabled.
The address of an interrupt status register
A mask. If a read of address intrstatusreg has any of the bits that are 1 in the mask set, the ISR will be called. If not, it will be skipped.
The interrupt handler. Must be NULL when an interrupt of level >3 is requested, because these types of interrupts aren't C-callable.
Optional argument for passed to the interrupt handler
Pointer to an intr_handle_t pointer to store a handle that can later be used to request details or free the interrupt. Can be NULL if no handle is required.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if the combination of arguments is invalid. -ENODEV No free interrupt found with the specified flags 0 otherwise

detaileddescription.para: Use an interrupt handle to disable the interrupt and release the resources associated with it. If the current core is not the core that registered this interrupt, this routine will be assigned to the core that allocated this interrupt, blocking and waiting until the resource is successfully released.

simplesect.para: When the handler shares its source with other handlers, the interrupt status bits it's responsible for should be managed properly before freeing it. See esp_intr_disable for more details. Please do not call this function in esp_ipc_call_blocking.

parameterdescription.para: The handle, as obtained by esp_intr_alloc or esp_intr_alloc_intrstatus

briefdescription.para: Get CPU number an interrupt is tied to.

parameterdescription.para: The handle, as obtained by esp_intr_alloc or esp_intr_alloc_intrstatus

simplesect.para: The core number where the interrupt is allocated

briefdescription.para: Get the allocated interrupt for a certain handle.

parameterdescription.para: The handle, as obtained by esp_intr_alloc or esp_intr_alloc_intrstatus

briefdescription.para: Disable the interrupt associated with the handle.

listitem.para: For local interrupts (ESP_INTERNAL_* sources), this function has to be called on the CPU the interrupt is allocated on. Other interrupts have no such restriction.

When several handlers sharing a same interrupt source, interrupt status bits, which are handled in the handler to be disabled, should be masked before the disabling, or handled in other enabled interrupts properly. Miss of interrupt status handling will cause infinite interrupt calls and finally system crash.

parameterdescription.para: The handle, as obtained by esp_intr_alloc or esp_intr_alloc_intrstatus

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if the combination of arguments is invalid. 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Enable the interrupt associated with the handle.

simplesect.para: For local interrupts (ESP_INTERNAL_* sources), this function has to be called on the CPU the interrupt is allocated on. Other interrupts have no such restriction.

parameterdescription.para: The handle, as obtained by esp_intr_alloc or esp_intr_alloc_intrstatus

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if the combination of arguments is invalid. 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Set the "in IRAM" status of the handler.

simplesect.para: Does not work on shared interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: The handle, as obtained by esp_intr_alloc or esp_intr_alloc_intrstatus
Whether the handler associated with this handle resides in IRAM. Handlers residing in IRAM can be called when cache is disabled.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if the combination of arguments is invalid. 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Disable interrupts that aren't specifically marked as running from IRAM.
